summary: Fun Spring‑85 Spectrum show with strong playing despite a shaky Garcia vocal, recorded on an unknown source of decent quality
review: The concert is remembered for its short, sweet first set and a high‑energy groove (notably the “1/2 Step” sequence) with Phil Lesh getting big kudos. Jerry’s voice was noticeably ragged – a common theme for the Spring ’85 run – but his guitar work remained sharp; this vocal issue keeps some listeners from rating it higher. No concrete recording details are given, but the lack of complaints suggests an acceptable, if not outstanding, source.

ai_rating.rationale: Two reviews give a mixed picture – strong musical performance offset by vocal problems and no clear info on recording fidelity, leading to an overall solid but not exceptional rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Reviewers do not comment on soundboard vs. audience or clarity; absence of complaints implies the audio is at least listenable.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set described as short and sweet with a great groove; second set suffered from vocal breakdown but the band kept the energy high.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Voice deteriorated and sounded ragged, but guitar playing remained sharp throughout.
band_member_comments.Phil: Great performance on the "1/2 Step" segment.
